text.title: In-Issue Ad Format Library Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every in-issue ad rotation must enforce. Hard rules; designed to prevent banner-fatigue, dedicated-send over-use, no-baseline measurement, and reader-trust erosion.

rule.statement: Only the 4 named formats are used: top_banner, soft_ps, mid_issue_case, dedicated_send. No bespoke "mid-paragraph link drop" or "footer ad" without adding it to the library and ratifying its cadence.

rationale.source: Newsletter A/B-test literature on format consistency

rule.rationale: Format proliferation destroys baseline math — without comparable formats, CTR/CVR figures are noise.

rule.statement: Per-format cadence caps: top_banner max 1×/issue; soft_ps max 3×/4 issues; mid_issue_case max 1×/2 issues; dedicated_send max 1×/6 issues. Caps measured across a sliding 6-issue window.
rule.rationale: Exceeding cadence caps triggers unsubscribes; the caps were derived from observed unsubscribe spikes in indie newsletters.

rule.statement: CTR + CVR for each format are measured against the founder's own 90-day baseline (not industry averages). Format scores tag as below_baseline / at_baseline / above_baseline.
rule.rationale: Industry CTR varies 0.5%-8% by niche; only baseline-relative scores are actionable for the founder.

rule.statement: Across any 6-issue window, at least 1 issue carries zero self-promo (format='none'). Excludes lead-magnet CTAs that exchange value for email.
rule.rationale: Always-on self-promo accelerates list decay; periodic empty-of-promo issues reset the trust budget.

rule.statement: Every ad has a typed cta_destination: product_paywall | waitlist | lead_magnet | feature_announcement. Mixed-purpose CTAs are forbidden (single button = single destination).
rule.rationale: Mixed CTAs underperform single-purpose CTAs by 40-60% in newsletter A/B tests.

rule.statement: Monthly: compute CTR + CVR per format vs baseline. Promote the top format (give it +1 slot next month within cadence caps); demote the worst (remove from rotation for 1 month). Log to the library file.
rule.rationale: Without monthly rotation, the worst-performing format keeps getting used by inertia.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 6-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before sending the next issue.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree run leaf for canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree skip leaf for non-applicable inputs.
